idea-plugin.name: Profiles
idea-plugin.description:
Group your projects into named sets — Work, Home, a client, a side project — or whatever you want to name them, and switch between them from one dropdown in the main toolbar. Pick a profile, and it opens that profile's projects, each in its own window, and closes the ones that aren't in it, so your open windows always match whatever you're working on. — it uses the project tabs feature in your IDE.

It's a profile switcher in the plain sense: many projects per profile, and you switch to them by switching profiles. These are your real, separate projects — never merged into one window — so run configurations, indexes, branches and open files are all kept exactly as you left them.

Where you use it

  • In the editor — the Profiles dropdown sits in the main toolbar, next to the project and branch widgets.
  • On the Welcome screen — a "New Profile Workspace" button builds a profile and opens its projects before you've opened anything.

Features

  • Group any number of projects into a profile.
  • One click switches the whole set: opens the profile's projects, closes the rest.
  • Give each profile a color and an icon (a built-in icon or your own emoji), shown in the toolbar.
  • Create a profile from the projects you already have open, or by picking folders.
  • Manage profiles — rename, add or remove projects, delete — under Settings > Tools > Profiles.
  • Light on memory: only the active profile stays open.

Open source under GPL-3.0 — github.com/ArcaneArts/Intelij-Profiles.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
• Profiles dropdown in the main toolbar and a "New Profile Workspace" button on the Welcome screen.
  • Switch a profile to open its projects and close the rest, in one click.
  • Create profiles from your open projects or by picking folders; manage them in Settings.

action.description: Pick several project folders and open them as a new profile
